content.text: Now I have a full picture. Let me rewrite cleanly — dropping the manual state, importing the accordion, and styling `AccordionTrigger` to match the existing sidebar label look.

content.content: The file /Users/jeevanpillay/Code/@lightfastai/lightfast/apps/app/src/components/app-sidebar.tsx has been updated successfully.

lines: +            {/* Manage Section - collapsible via Radix Accordion */}
+                  <AccordionTrigger className="h-8 px-2 py-0 text-xs font-medium text-muted-foreground hover:text-foreground hover:no-underline [&>svg]:size-3">

content.text:
Key changes:
- Replaced manual `useState` + `toggleGroup` with `Accordion type="multiple" defaultValue={["manage"]}` — open by default, no persistence
- `AccordionContent` uses Radix's built-in `animate-accordion-up/down` for smooth height animation
- `AccordionTrigger` styled to match the existing label look (`h-8 px-2 text-xs text-muted-foreground`) with `hover:no-underline` to suppress the default underline behavior
- `[&>svg]:size-3` shrinks the built-in chevron to match the sidebar scale
- Dropped `SidebarGroupLabel` import since it's no longer used

lines: {/* Manage Section - collapsible via Radix Accordion */}
-                  <AccordionTrigger className="h-8 px-2 py-0 text-xs font-medium text-muted-foreground hover:text-foreground hover:no-underline [&>svg]:size-3">

content.text:
Key changes:
- `AccordionPrimitive` used directly — `SidebarMenuButton asChild` composes with `AccordionPrimitive.Trigger`, so the button gets Radix's `data-state` attribute and all sidebar button styling
- `ChevronDown` is the first child (left of "Manage"), rotates to `-90deg` when closed via `[&[data-state=closed]>svg:first-child]:rotate-[-90deg]`
- `size="sm"` gives `text-sm` + `h-7` sizing
- Smooth height animation via `animate-accordion-up/down` on the content

message.content:
question should this primitive be actually part of @packages/ui/src/components/ui/sidebar.tsx 

perhaps, we need to rework how our sidebar.tsx is structured instead of doing this on the actual app-level

so imagine if sidebar-group was ltiterally this accordion-like component with a title.

content.text:
Yes, exactly right. The accordion behavior is a UI concern — it belongs in the component library, not the app. The app shouldn't need to know about `AccordionPrimitive` at all.

The ideal API would look something like:

```tsx
// app-sidebar.tsx — clean consumer
<SidebarGroup label="Manage" collapsible defaultOpen>
  <SidebarMenu>
    <NavItems ... />
  </SidebarMenu>
</SidebarGroup>
```

And `sidebar.tsx` handles the Radix composition internally. Groups without `label` or `collapsible` stay as-is (no behavior change for the primary nav group).

**Trade-offs to consider before reworking:**

- `SidebarGroup` currently renders a plain `div` — wrapping it in `AccordionPrimitive.Root/Item` changes the DOM structure, which could affect existing consumers
- The `collapsible` prop makes the label a `SidebarMenuButton`-styled trigger — that's a different element type than the current `SidebarGroupLabel`, so the label prop needs to handle both cases
- `defaultOpen` should default to `true` so existing labeled groups don't collapse by default

**Scope of the change:** modify `SidebarGroup` in `packages/ui/src/components/ui/sidebar.tsx` to accept optional `label`, `collapsible`, and `defaultOpen` props, internalize the `AccordionPrimitive` composition, and then revert `app-sidebar.tsx` back to a clean consumer.

Worth doing if you expect more sidebar groups to need this behavior. Want me to go ahead with the rework?

Now I have the full picture. I'll modify `SidebarGroup` in the UI package to accept `label`, `collapsible`, and `defaultOpen` props — internalizing the accordion — then clean up `app-sidebar.tsx` to use the simple API.

content.text:
Done. Summary of what changed:

**`packages/ui/src/components/ui/sidebar.tsx`**
- `SidebarGroup` now accepts `label`, `collapsible`, and `defaultOpen` props
- When `collapsible + label`: renders a `SidebarMenuButton`-styled `AccordionPrimitive.Trigger` with a left-side `ChevronDown` that rotates to `-90deg` when closed, wrapping children in animated `AccordionPrimitive.Content`
- When `label` only: renders a passive `SidebarGroupLabel` (existing behavior, no regression)
- When neither: renders exactly as before (plain div wrapper)

**`apps/app/src/components/app-sidebar.tsx`**
- No `AccordionPrimitive`, no `ChevronDown`, no manual state — just `<SidebarGroup label="Manage" collapsible defaultOpen>`

message.content:
very nice. final update to sidebar.tsx,

we want hover and active to be different colors.

hover use /50 opacity reduce

content.text: `tailwind-merge` via `cn()` handles the override — just pass `hover:bg-sidebar-accent/50` in the className and it wins over the variant's `hover:bg-sidebar-accent`.

content.content: The file /Users/jeevanpillay/Code/@lightfastai/lightfast/packages/ui/src/components/ui/sidebar.tsx has been updated successfully.

content.text: `hover:bg-sidebar-accent/50` overrides the variant's `hover:bg-sidebar-accent` via `tailwind-merge`, while `data-[active=true]:bg-sidebar-accent` (full opacity) remains untouched for the active state.

The issue is the base class also has `data-[state=open]:hover:bg-sidebar-accent` — that fires when the accordion is open (the default state), and `tailwind-merge` treats it as a different key so it doesn't get replaced by `hover:bg-sidebar-accent/50`. Need to override both:

content.text: All re-applied. The key addition this time is `data-[state=open]:hover:bg-sidebar-accent/50` alongside `hover:bg-sidebar-accent/50` — the base class has both `hover:bg-sidebar-accent` and `data-[state=open]:hover:bg-sidebar-accent`, so both need overriding to keep the `/50` opacity consistent regardless of whether the accordion is open or closed.
